name: Missippi Mud Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F (160 degrees C).
2. Grease and flour a 13x9x2-inch baking pan.
3. Cream shortening and sugar together in a mixing bowl until light and fluffy.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Blend in vanilla extract.
6. Sift flour, cocoa, and salt together; add to creamed mixture and blend thoroughly.
7. Stir in pecans.
8. Pour batter into prepared pan.
9. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until cake begins to pull away from sides of pan and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Remove pan from oven and sprinkle marshmallows evenly over cake.
11. Return to oven for 10 minutes or until marshmallows are melted.
12. Cool in pan on a wire rack.
13. Spread with Mississippi Mud Icing. Chill before cutting into squares.
Mississippi Mud Icing:
1 cup (2 sticks) butter or margarine, melted
1/3 cup cocoa powder
1/2 cup evaporated milk
4 cups sifted confectioners' sugar (about 1 pound)
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1/2 cup chopped pecans
Combine butter and cocoa in a mixing bowl; add evaporated milk. Gradually stir in sugar, beating until smooth. Stir in vanilla and pecans; blend thoroughly.
